# Forge AI Residency

The Forge AI Residency is a 7-day fully
residential AI program run
by LevelUp Learning. It is built for
founders, marketers, and operators who
want to learn how to build with AI by
shipping real products and the
operations that run them.

The program is structured around two
pillars: Product (2 days) and Operations
(3 days). Day 1 is orientation and the
psychology of storytelling. Day 7 is
Demo Day. Each builder leaves with two
shipped things: a working AI product
live on a real URL, and an operations
layer — automation, agent, knowledge,
voice — running a real function in
their business.

Edition 1 runs September 11–17, 2026.
Five online prep sessions
happen the week before, September 5–9.
Together, 5 online sessions + 7 days on
the ground = 12 days of program total.

20 builders per cohort. Hard limit. The
program is invite-only and applications
are reviewed within 48 hours by the
admissions team.

Mentors include Ankur Upadhyay
(Product) — Platform & AI Product
Leader with ML systems for 3M+
merchants at AB InBev and agentic AI
for 15,000+ users at o9 Solutions; and
Parth Bansal (Operations) — AI Staff
PM at the AI-healthcare frontier,
operational AI for multi-billion-dollar
hospital systems. Rahul Reddy hosts;
Vaibhav Kejriwal (n8n Bangalore
Ambassador), Kevin Adams (LinkedIn
Design Top Voice), and Sabilashan
Ganeshan (Country Lead at Perplexity)
also mentor and lead firesides.

Tools used in the program include
Claude, Claude Code, n8n, Supabase,
Vercel, Lovable, and MCP.

Fee: INR 1,50,000 all inclusive.
Included: residential stay on double
occupancy, all meals, both tracks
(Product + Operations), expert
faculty throughout the residency,
curated experiences during the
program, community access after the
program, and the Forge AI Goodie Bag.
Not included: travel to and from the
venue.

Application fee: INR 900 (refundable
if you're not accepted). The
application fee secures your interview
slot with the admissions team. Real
founders, marketers, and operators
with serious build intent are
prioritised.

Phase 1 — Online prep
(Sept 5–9, 5 sessions before travel):

  Session 1 (Sept 5): Orientation. Get
  every tool live. Build your business
  "second brain."

  Session 2 (Sept 6): Product Thinking
  Fundamentals. Where AI creates value,
  the AI-shaped problem filter, the
  build spec. With Ankur.

  Session 3 (Sept 7): Design
  Fundamentals. Visual hierarchy,
  layout, type, what makes a product
  feel trustworthy.

  Session 4 (Sept 8): Automation &
  Workflows 101. Triggers, steps, the
  think-act-observe loop. With Parth.

  Session 5 (Sept 9): Review & Align
  1-on-1. Present your build brief,
  get live mentor feedback, lock the
  plan.

Phase 2 — Seven days on the ground
(Sept 11–17):

  Day 1 (Sept 11): Arrival, orientation,
  and the psychology of storytelling
  with Rahul. Your Demo Day narrative
  framework starts here.

  Days 2–3 (Sept 12–13) · Product
  with Ankur: Spec it, make it
  intelligent, ship it, prove it. By
  end of Day 3 — Ship #1: a live AI
  product on a real URL with an eval
  scorecard.

  Days 4–6 (Sept 14–16) · Operations
  with Parth: Automate it then make it
  think. Connect, ground, voice. Full
  pipeline, multi-agent, human-in-the-
  loop, plus the outdoor hackathon
  and golden-hour showcase. By end of
  Day 6 — Ship #2: an operations
  layer (automation + agent + voice +
  knowledge) running a real function
  in your business.

  Day 7 (Sept 17): Demo Day. Eight
  minutes each, no slides, real
  working demo. Then commit your next
  three builds and your 90-day
  roadmap.

What you leave with: a working AI
product live on a URL, an operations
layer running a real function in your
business, a 90-day roadmap, and a
hand-picked cohort and mentor
network that keeps going long after
you leave.

# About LevelUp Learning

The Forge AI Residency is part of the
Forge program family run by LevelUp
Learning. LevelUp has been running
residential learning programs in India
for filmmakers, creators, writers, and
now AI builders. The Forge format —
mornings teach the concept, afternoons
build on your own business, mentors
circulating — is the same teach-then-do
format used across the residential
programs LevelUp has shipped.

# FAQ

Q: Who is this for?
A: Founders, marketers, operators, and
working professionals who want to
build with AI. No coding background
needed.

Q: Do I need to know how to code?
A: No. Neither of the two tracks
requires coding. If you can write a
brief, you can build with these tools.

Q: How many people are in each cohort?
A: 20. Hard limit.

Q: What is the application fee for?
A: It books your interview slot and
signals intent. It is separate from
the program fee and refundable if
you are not accepted.

Q: I have a full-time job. Can I
attend?
A: Yes. Most participants are working
professionals. You take a fixed block
of time off and come back with
something done.

Q: What if I have never used AI tools
before?
A: The 5 pre-program online prep
sessions bring you up to speed. You
arrive ready to build, not to catch up.

Q: What do I actually leave with?
A: A working AI product live on a URL,
a live automation that runs a real
function in your business, a 90-day
roadmap, and a network of 20 serious
builders.

Q: Why pay for this when free AI
content exists everywhere?
A: You have probably already found
the free content. The question is
whether it has actually moved
anything forward for you. The Forge
is for people who are done watching
and ready to build.
